title: Swing anchor + rotation-sign decode (must land before per-pixel Z hook)
goal: Movements/Swing 12.89 dB. The per-pixel Z-buffer plumbing is ALREADY LANDED (perspective.ts::projectQuadWithWorldZ + renderPerspectiveQuadDepth + CAMERA_Z_DEFAULT, unit-tested by perspective.test.ts). Depth math is verified: smaller world-Z wins pixels. What BLOCKS hooking it in Swing's render path (renderChildLayers) is upstream geometry — reproduced empirically on T-qswing00001: with the CURRENT engine worldTransforms, a per-pixel depth composite of the 2 clone children lands at 12.63 dB (−0.26 vs 12.89 baseline), and a whole-quad z-sort lands at 12.58 (−0.31). The FEED to the depth pass is wrong, not the compositor. TWO specific decode bugs to resolve here: (a) ANCHOR SELECTION — Swing.motr Anchor widget stores value=2 with menu tags [Right=0, Left=1, Top=2, Bottom=3], so 2 means "Top". But my probe of Movements__Swing f10 shows BOTH clones with anchor=(0, 540, 0), which in Motion Y-DOWN is frame BOTTOM (not Top). Verify the rig-widget path: parser/rig.ts + evaluator/index.ts anchor resolution, and whether the value-remap analogous to Push's factoryID-13 tag-vs-index bug applies here. (b) ROTATION-SIGN — evaluator/index.ts:311 negates  (calibrated for Fall/top-back tilt). On Swing this makes both clones matrix-rotate in the SAME direction as the Ramp progresses (not opposite as the two-sided-card pattern requires). At f10 my probe measured Clone A matrix θ ≈ -33° and Clone B ≈ +57°; the difference IS π/2 (correct) but the relationship to camera-facing is inverted vs the GUI GT (GT shows Photo B occupying top ~55% of frame at f10; engine renders A almost fully covering the frame). Once these decodes ship, hook the pair in renderChildLayers by (i) restoring detectTwoSidedCard in geometry.ts (2 clone children, static perpendicular rotationX, distinct cloneSourceIds — see prior WIP commit) and (ii) calling projectQuadWithWorldZ + renderPerspectiveQuadDepth on a shared Float64Array zbuf initialised to Infinity, in place of the current painter's-order children loop. DoD: Swing measurably up (>0.3 dB target), gate 0 regressions, no-hardcode.test.ts registers the detector (fires on ≥2 built-ins — the two-sided-card pattern MUST be corpus-searched first).
